title: Colosseum is completed
subtitle: Ancient Roman amphitheatre, a landmark of Rome, Italy
fact: The Colosseum is an elliptical amphitheatre in the centre of the city of Rome, Italy, just east of the Roman Forum.

title: Stonehenge is completed
subtitle: Neolithic henge monument in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, UK
fact: Stonehenge is a prehistoric megalithic structure on Salisbury Plain in Wiltshire, England, two miles (3 km) west of Amesbury.

title: Göbekli Tepe is completed
subtitle: Neolithic archaeological site in Turkey
fact: Göbekli Tepe is a Neolithic archaeological site in Upper Mesopotamia (al-Jazira) in modern-day Turkey.

title: Pompeii is completed
subtitle: Ancient Roman city near modern Naples, Italy
fact: Pompeii was a city in what is now the municipality of Pompei, near Naples, in the Campania region of Italy.

title: Terracotta Army is completed
subtitle: Collection of ancient Chinese military statues
fact: The Terracotta Army is a collection of terracotta sculptures depicting the armies of Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.

title: Petra is completed
subtitle: Ancient rock-cut historical city in Jordan
fact: Petra, originally known to its inhabitants as Raqmu, is an ancient city and archaeological site in southern Jordan.

title: Persepolis is completed
subtitle: Ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Empire, Iranian national heritage site
fact: Persepolis was the ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Empire.

title: Western Wall is completed
subtitle: Holy site of Judaism in Jerusalem
fact: The Western Wall is an ancient retaining wall of the built-up hill known to Jews and Christians as the Temple Mount of Jerusalem.

title: Chichen Itza is completed
subtitle: Pre-Columbian Maya city in Mexico
fact: Chichen Itza was a large pre-Columbian city built by the Maya people of the Terminal Classic period.

title: The Great Sphinx is completed
subtitle: Ancient Egyptian monumental sculpture
fact: The Great Sphinx of Giza is a limestone statue of a reclining sphinx, a mythical creature with the head of a human and the body of a lion.

title: Ur is completed
subtitle: Ancient Mesopotamian city-state
fact: Ur was a major Sumerian city-state in ancient Mesopotamia, located at the site of modern Tell el-Muqayyar in Dhi Qar Governorate, Iraq.

title: Temple of Ephesian Artemis is completed
subtitle: Temple in Ephesus, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World
fact: The Temple of Artemis or Artemision, also known as the Temple of Diana, was a Greek temple dedicated to a localised form of the goddess Artemis.

title: Tel Megiddo is completed
subtitle: Site of an ancient city in northern Israel's Jezreel valley
fact: Tel Megiddo, is the site of the ancient city of Megiddo, the remains of which form a tell or archaeological mound, situated in northern Israel at the western edge of the Jezreel Valley.

title: Nazca Lines is completed
subtitle: Large geoglyphs made in the soil of the Nazca Desert in Peru
fact: The Nazca lines are a group of geoglyphs made in the soil of the Nazca Desert in southern Peru.

title: Susa is completed
subtitle: Ancient city in Iran, World Heritage site in Iran
fact: Susa was an ancient city in the lower Zagros Mountains about 250 km (160 mi) east of the Tigris, between the Karkheh and Dez Rivers in Iran.

title: Ctesiphon is completed
subtitle: Capital of the Iranian empire in the Parthian and Sasanian eras in present Iraq
fact: Ctesiphon was an ancient city in modern Iraq, on the eastern bank of the Tigris, about 35 kilometres (22 mi) southeast of Baghdad.

title: Çatalhöyük is completed
subtitle: Archaeological site in Konya Province, Turkey
fact: Çatalhöyük is a tell of a very large Neolithic and Chalcolithic proto-city settlement in southern Anatolia, which existed from approximately 7500 BC to 5600 BC and flourished around 7000 BC.

title: Roman Forum is completed
subtitle: Ancient Roman center of the city, a landmark of Rome, Italy
fact: The Roman Forum, also known by its Latin name Forum Romanum, is a rectangular forum (plaza) surrounded by the ruins of several important ancient government buildings at the centre of the city of Rome.

title: Abu Simbel is completed
subtitle: UNESCO World Heritage Site in southern Egypt
fact: Abu Simbel is a historic site comprising two massive rock-cut temples in the village of Abu Simbel, Aswan Governorate, Upper Egypt, near the border with Sudan.

title: Circus Maximus is completed
subtitle: Ancient Roman circus in Rome
fact: The Circus Maximus is an ancient Roman chariot-racing stadium and mass entertainment venue in Rome, Italy.

title: Thebes is completed
subtitle: Ancient Egyptian city
fact: Thebes, known to the ancient Egyptians as Waset, was an ancient Egyptian city located along the Nile about 800 kilometers (500 mi) south of the Mediterranean.

title: Pyramid of Djoser is completed
subtitle: Archeological site in Egypt
fact: The Pyramid of Djoser, sometimes called the Step Pyramid of Djoser or Step Pyramid of Horus Netjerikhet, is an archaeological site in the Saqqara necropolis, Egypt, northwest of the ruins of Memphis.

title: Karnak Temple Complex is completed
subtitle: Ancient Egyptian temple complex
fact: The Karnak Temple Complex, commonly known as Karnak, comprises a vast mix of temples, pylons, chapels, and other buildings near Luxor, Egypt.

title: Arch of Titus is completed
subtitle: Triumphal arch in Rome
fact: The Arch of Titus is a 1st-century AD honorific arch, located on the Via Sacra, Rome, just to the south-east of the Roman Forum.

title: Skara Brae is completed
subtitle: Neolithic archaeological site in Scotland
fact: Skara Brae is a stone-built Neolithic settlement, located on the Bay of Skaill in the parish of Sandwick, on the west coast of Mainland, the largest island in the Orkney archipelago of Scotland.

title: Cyrene is completed
subtitle: Ancient Greek and Roman city near present-day Shahhat, Libya
fact: Cyrene, also sometimes anglicized as Kyrene, was an ancient Greek colony and Roman city near present-day Shahhat in northeastern Libya in North Africa.

title: Capernaum is completed
subtitle: Village at Lake Tiberias in the north of historical Judea, associated with Jesus
fact: Capernaum was a fishing village established during the time of the Hasmoneans, located on the northern shore of the Sea of Galilee.

title: Trajan's Column is completed
subtitle: Ancient Roman victory column, a landmark of Rome, Italy
fact: Trajan's Column is a Roman triumphal column in Rome, Italy, that commemorates Roman emperor Trajan's victory in the Dacian Wars.

title: Mausoleum of Augustus is completed
subtitle: Building in Campo Marzio, Italy
fact: The Mausoleum of Augustus is a large tomb built by the Roman Emperor Augustus in 28 BC on the Campus Martius in Rome, Italy.

title: Arch of Constantine is completed
subtitle: Triumphal arch in Rome
fact: The Arch of Constantine is a triumphal arch in Rome dedicated to the emperor Constantine the Great.

title: Miletus is completed
subtitle: Ancient city of Ionia, in modern-day Turkey
fact: Miletus was an influential ancient Greek city on the western coast of Anatolia, near the mouth of the Maeander River in present day Turkey.

title: Mehrgarh is completed
subtitle: Neolithic archaeological site in Balochistan, Pakistan
fact: Mehrgarh is a Neolithic archaeological site situated on the Kacchi Plain of Balochistan in Pakistan.

title: Ostia is completed
subtitle: Ancient Roman city and colony
fact: Ostia Antica is the modern name given to the ancient Roman city of Ostia.

title: Baths of Caracalla is completed
subtitle: Public baths in ancient Rome
fact: The Baths of Caracalla in Rome, Italy, were the city's second largest Roman public baths, or thermae, after the Baths of Diocletian.

title: Domus Aurea is completed
subtitle: Domus, palace of Roman emperor Nero, "Golden house"
fact: The Domus Aurea was a vast landscaped complex built by the Emperor Nero largely on the Oppian Hill in the heart of ancient Rome after the great fire in 64 AD had destroyed a large part of the city.

title: Edessa is completed
subtitle: Ancient city in upper Mesopotamia, modern day Urfa, Southeast Turkey
fact: Edessa was an ancient city (polis) in Upper Mesopotamia, in what is now Urfa or Şanlıurfa, Turkey.

title: Megalithic Temples of Malta is completed
subtitle: Several prehistoric temples on the Maltese Islands
fact: The Megalithic Temples of Malta are several prehistoric temples, some of which are UNESCO World Heritage Sites, built during three distinct periods approximately between 3600 BC and 2500 BC on the island country of Malta.

title: Erechtheion is completed
subtitle: Ancient Greek temple on the north side of the Acropolis of Athens
fact: The Erechtheion or Temple of Athena Polias is an ancient Greek Ionic temple, on the north side of the Acropolis, Athens, that was primarily dedicated to the goddess Athena.

title: Etchmiadzin Cathedral is completed
subtitle: Cultural heritage monument of Armenia
fact: Etchmiadzin Cathedral is the mother church of the Armenian Apostolic Church, located in the city dually known as Etchmiadzin (Ejmiatsin) and Vagharshapat, Armenia.

title: Bagan is completed
subtitle: Ancient city in Myanmar
fact: Bagan is an ancient city and a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the Mandalay Region of Myanmar.

title: Halicarnassus is completed
subtitle: Ancient Greek city in Caria, present day Bodrum in Turkey
fact: Halicarnassus was an ancient Greek city in Caria, in Anatolia.

title: Red Pyramid is completed
subtitle: Smooth-sided pyramid
fact: The Red Pyramid, also called the North Pyramid, is the largest of the pyramids located at the Dahshur necropolis in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Tower of Hercules is completed
subtitle: Ancient lighthouse in Spain
fact: The Tower of Hercules is the oldest known extant Roman lighthouse.

title: Aurelian Walls is completed
subtitle: Historic line of walls built around the city of Rome, Italy
fact: The Aurelian Walls are a line of city walls built between 271 AD and 275 AD in Rome, Italy, during the reign of the Roman Emperor Aurelian.

title: Paestum is completed
subtitle: Ancient Greek city in present-day village Paestum, Italy
fact: Paestum was a major ancient Greek city on the coast of the Tyrrhenian Sea, in Magna Graecia.

title: Qumran is completed
subtitle: Archaeological site in the Palestinian Authority, West Bank
fact: Qumran is an archaeological site in the West Bank managed by Israel's Qumran National Park.

title: Mari is completed
subtitle: Ancient Sumerian and Amorite city
fact: Mari was an ancient Semitic city-state in modern-day Syria.

title: Amarna is completed
subtitle: Egyptian archaeological site
fact: Amarna is an extensive ancient Egyptian archaeological site containing the ruins of Akhetaten, the capital city during the late Eighteenth Dynasty.

title: Leptis Magna is completed
subtitle: Ancient city in modern Libya
fact: Leptis or Lepcis Magna, also known by other names in antiquity, was a prominent city of the Carthaginian Empire and Roman Libya at the mouth of the Wadi Lebda in the Mediterranean.

title: Luxor Temple is completed
subtitle: Ancient Egyptian tem
fact: The Luxor Temple is a large Ancient Egyptian temple complex located on the east bank of the Nile River in the city today known as Luxor and was constructed approximately 1400 BCE.

title: Bent Pyramid is completed
subtitle: Ancient tomb in Egypt
fact: The Bent Pyramid is an ancient Egyptian pyramid located at the royal necropolis of Dahshur, approximately 40 kilometres (25 mi) south of Cairo, built under the Old Kingdom Pharaoh Sneferu.

title: Pyramid of Menkaure is completed
subtitle: Smallest of the three main Pyramids of Giza, Egypt
fact: The pyramid of Menkaure is the smallest of the three main pyramids of the Giza pyramid complex, located on the Giza Plateau in the southwestern outskirts of Cairo, Egypt.

title: Nicomedia is completed
subtitle: Ancient city of Bithynia in modern-day Turkey
fact: Nicomedia was an ancient Greek city located in what is now Turkey.

title: Ġgantija is completed
subtitle: Megalithic temple complex from the Neolithic on the Mediterranean island of Gozo
fact: Ġgantija is a megalithic temple complex from the Neolithic era, on the Mediterranean island of Gozo in Malta.

title: Seleucia is completed
subtitle: Capital of the Seleucid Empire
fact: Seleucia, also known as Seleucia-on-Tigris or Seleucia on the Tigris or Seleucia ad Tigrim, was a major Mesopotamian city, located on the west bank of the Tigris River within the present-day Baghdad Governorate in Iraq.

title: Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus is completed
subtitle: The poliadic temple of the ancient city of Rome
fact: The Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus, also known as the Temple of Jupiter Capitolinus, was the most important temple in Ancient Rome, located on the Capitoline Hill.

title: Dura-Europos is completed
subtitle: Ancient Syrian city
fact: Dura-Europos was a Hellenistic, Parthian, and Roman border city built on an escarpment 90 metres above the southwestern bank of the Euphrates river.

title: Lothal is completed
subtitle: Prominent city of the ancient Indus valley civilization
fact: Lothal was one of the southernmost sites of the ancient Indus Valley civilisation, located in the Bhal region of the Indian state of Gujarat.

title: Basilica of San Lorenzo is completed
subtitle: Church in Florence, Italy
fact: The Basilica di San Lorenzo is one of the largest churches of Florence, Italy, situated at the centre of the main market district of the city, and it is the burial place of all the principal members of the Medici family from Cosimo il Vecchio to Cosimo III.

title: Anshan Persia is completed
subtitle: Ancient city of Elam
fact: Anshan modern Tall-e Malyan, also Tall-i Malyan, was an Elamite and ancient Persian city.

title: Great Mosque of Gaza is completed
subtitle: Mosque in Gaza City, Palestine
fact: The Great Mosque of Gaza, also known as the Great Omari Mosque, is a mosque in a ruinous state, located in Gaza City, in the State of Palestine.

title: Temple of Caesar is completed
subtitle: Building in Roman Forum, Italy
fact: The Temple of Caesar or Temple of Divus Iulius, also known as Temple of the Deified Julius Caesar, delubrum, heroon or Temple of the Comet Star, was an ancient structure in the Roman Forum of Rome, Italy, located near the Regia and the Temple of Vesta.

title: Hagia Irene is completed
subtitle: Byzantine church building in Istanbul, Turkey now a hall and museum
fact: Hagia Irene or Hagia Eirene, sometimes known also as Saint Irene, is a former Eastern Orthodox church located in the outer courtyard of Topkapı Palace in Istanbul.

title: Garni Temple is completed
subtitle: Sacred architecture
fact: The Garni Temple is a classical colonnaded structure in the village of Garni, in central Armenia, around 30 km (19 mi) east of Yerevan.

title: Antonia Fortress is completed
subtitle: Military barracks in Jerusalem
fact: The Antonia Fortress was a citadel built by Herod the Great and named for Herod's patron Mark Antony, as a fortress whose chief function was to protect the Second Temple.

title: Asyut is completed
subtitle: City in Egypt
fact: Asyut is a city in Upper Egypt and the capital of the modern Asyut Governorate in Egypt.

title: Lyceum is completed
subtitle: Public meeting place in Classical Athens
fact: The Lyceum was a temple in Athens dedicated to Apollo Lyceus.

title: Palace of Darius in Susa is completed
subtitle: Palace in Shush, Iran
fact: The Palace of Darius in Susa was a palace complex that was built at the site of Susa, Iran, during the reign of Darius I over the Achaemenid Empire.

title: San Pietro in Vincoli is completed
subtitle: Church in Rome
fact: San Pietro in Vincoli is a Roman Catholic titular church and minor basilica in Rome, Italy.

title: Brú na Bóinne is completed
subtitle: Archaeological site
fact: Brú na Bóinne, also called the Boyne Valley tombs, is an ancient monument complex and ritual landscape in County Meath, Ireland, located in a bend of the River Boyne.

title: Carchemish is completed
subtitle: Ancient city in Turkey / Syria
fact: Carchemish, also spelled Karkemish, was an important ancient capital in the northern part of the region of Syria.

title: Takht-e Soleyman is completed
subtitle: Archaeological site in West Azarbaijan, Iranian national heritage site
fact: Takht-e Soleymān, is an archaeological site in West Azerbaijan, Iran dating back to the Sasanian Empire.

title: Serapeum of Alexandria is completed
subtitle: Temple in Alexandria
fact: The Serapeum of Alexandria was an ancient Greek temple built by Ptolemy III Euergetes and dedicated to the Greco-Egyptian syncretic god Serapis, made the protector of Alexandria, Egypt.

title: Santa Cecilia in Trastevere is completed
subtitle: Church in Rome
fact: Santa Cecilia in Trastevere is a titular church and minor basilica located in Trastevere, Rome.

title: Ka'ba-ye Zartosht is completed
subtitle: Stepped structure in the Naqsh-e Rustam compound in Fars, Iran
fact: Kaaba-ye Zartosht, also called the Kaaba or Cube of Zarathustra, is a rectangular stepped stone structure in the Naqsh-e Rustam compound beside Zangiabad village in Marvdasht county in Fars, Iran.

title: Dur-Sharrukin is completed
subtitle: Assyrian capital in the time of Sargon II
fact: Dur-Sharrukin, present day Khorsabad, was the Assyrian capital in the time of Sargon II of Assyria.

title: Acrocorinth is completed
subtitle: Fortified rocky hill, the acropolis of the ancient city of Corinth, Greece
fact: Acrocorinth is a monolithic rock overlooking the ancient city of Corinth, Greece.

title: Dahshur is completed
subtitle: Village in Giza Governorate, Egypt
fact: Dahshur is an ancient Egyptian pyramid complex and necropolis and shares the name of the nearby village of Manshiyyat Dahshur in markaz Badrashin, Giza.

title: Temple of Aphaea is completed
subtitle: Ancient Greek temple on Aigina
fact: The Temple of Aphaia is an Ancient Greek temple located within a sanctuary complex dedicated to the goddess Aphaia on the island of Aegina, which lies in the Saronic Gulf.

title: Apamea is completed
subtitle: Ancient city in Al-Suqaylabiyah, Syria
fact: Apamea, on the right bank of the Orontes River, was an ancient Greek and Roman city.

title: Sicyon is completed
subtitle: Ancient Greek city state Sicyon situated in the northern Peloponnesus
fact: Sicyon or Sikyōn was an ancient Greek city state situated in the northern Peloponnesus between Corinth and Achaea on the territory of the present-day regional unit of Corinthia.

title: Temple of Hera, Olympia is completed
subtitle: Temple in Greece
fact: The Temple of Hera, or Heraion, is an ancient Archaic Greek temple at Olympia, Greece, that was dedicated to Hera, queen of the Greek gods.

title: Tachara is completed
subtitle: Palace in Persepolis, Iran
fact: The Tachara, or the Tachar Château, also referred to as the Palace of Darius the Great, was the exclusive building of Darius I at Persepolis, Iran.

title: Khirokitia is completed
subtitle: Archaeological site in Cyprus
fact: Khirokitia is an archaeological site on the island of Cyprus dating from the Neolithic age.

title: Church of Saint Simeon Stylites is completed
subtitle: Historical church building located to the northwest of Aleppo, Syria
fact: The Church of Saint Simeon Stylites is one of the oldest surviving church complexes, founded in the 5th century.

title: Temple of Peace is completed
subtitle: Temple dedicated to the goddess Pax in ancient Rome
fact: The Temple of Peace, also known as the Forum of Vespasian, was built in Rome in 71 AD under Emperor Vespasian in honour to Pax, the Roman goddess of peace.

title: Old Temple of Athena is completed
subtitle: Ancient temple on the Athenian Acropolis
fact: The Old Temple of Athena or the Archaios Neos was an archaic Greek limestone Doric temple on the Acropolis of Athens probably built in the second half of the sixth-century BCE, and which housed the xoanon of Athena Polias.

title: Basilica of San Lorenzo is completed
subtitle: Church building in Milan, Italy
fact: The Basilica of San Lorenzo Maggiore is a Roman Catholic church in Milan, Northern Italy.

title: St. Nicholas Church, Demre is completed
subtitle: Ancient East Roman basilica church
fact: St.

title: Temple of Venus Genetrix is completed
subtitle: The main temple in the Forum of Julius Caesar at Rome
fact: The Temple of Venus Genetrix is a ruined temple in the Forum of Caesar, Rome, dedicated to the Roman goddess Venus Genetrix, the founding goddess of the Julian gens.

title: Palace of Ardashir is completed
subtitle: Royal palace of ancient Persia
fact: The Palace of Ardashir Bābakān, also known as the Ātashkadeh, is a castle located on the slopes of the mountain on which Qal'eh Dokhtar is situated.

title: Church of the Acheiropoietos is completed
subtitle: Church in Thessaloniki, Greece
fact: The Church of the Acheiropoietos is a 5th-century Byzantine-era Greek Orthodox church, located in the city of Thessaloniki, Central Macedonia, in northern Greece.

title: Monastery of the Holy Cross is completed
subtitle: Monastery in Jerusalem, Israel
fact: The Monastery of the Cross is an Eastern Orthodox monastery near the Nayot neighborhood of Jerusalem.
